element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Base offensive capability, present from start
Four-level speed control, foundational to dodging patterns
Companion fire units, auto-populate to max of 2
New exclusive weapons, lost on destruction on higher difficulties
Signature visual threat, intricate patterns and hit zones
Escalating enemy density and attack complexity per planet
Advanced enemy patterns, removal of cloaking device threats

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Escalating difficulty curve from planet selection stages (1-5) into intense headquarters gauntlet (6-8); described as 'extremely difficult' and suited for 'hardcore shooter fans'; wave-based tension with brief relief periods between enemy formations

difficulty_curve.early_game: Moderate; player choice of initial five planets allows some difficulty selection; introduction of speed mechanics and CLAW system enables learning curve
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalating; planet stages (1-5) increase enemy complexity and density as player progresses through each selected world
difficulty_curve.late_game: Severe; headquarters stages (6-8) represent significant difficulty spike with advanced enemy patterns, Cerberus system encounters, and final boss battle
notable_spikes: Transition from planet stages (5) to headquarters stages (6)
Stage 8 final confrontation with Cha Os bio-computer

notable_level_patterns.description: Five selectable planets (Hydra, Gorgon, Seiren, Haides, Ellis) allow non-linear stage progression; each planet features distinct enemy patterns and visual themes
Shift from overhead free-directional format of previous games to continuous horizontal scroll; became the new series standard
Intricate layered backgrounds with parallax scrolling provide visual depth and create pacing variation; praised for aesthetic impact
Final three stages (6-8) form continuous difficulty escalation into enemy stronghold; removal of stage selection, forced progression through ORN defenses to Cha Os encounter
